title: Veterinary bacteriology in Denmark from the 1880s to 2022.
author_string: Leisner, Jørgen J, Larsen, Jens Laurits
journal_full: APMIS : acta pathologica, microbiologica, et immunologica Scandinavica
abstract: This paper gives an account of the history of veterinary bacteriology including clinical veterinary bacteriology as well as the area of veterinary public health in Denmark from the 1880s to 2022. We describe key persons, including B. Bang, C.O. Jensen, K.A. Jensen and others who made important contributions to the development of these areas of microbiological expertise, and we discuss how challenges ranging from bovine tuberculosis to bacterial antimicrobial resistance have been met. Further, we describe progress in research on important bacterial pathogens both with regard to animal clinical aspects and zoonotic food-related aspects. Finally, we describe current issues in relation to One Health and research organization.
